summary: The most frequent side effect associated with phenytoin use is gingival hyperplasia based primarily due to consistent reports highlighting this condition as a significant concern across multiple studies examining pharmacological outcomes involving anticonvulsants like phenytoin. This conclusion aligns directly with established medical literature emphasizing oral health complications arising frequently alongside therapeutic benefits derived through administration of such agents targeting neurological disorders or seizures management scenarios where phenytoin plays an essential role despite potential risks including but not limited to gum overgrowth issues observed commonly during prolonged treatment courses utilizing these types of medication regimens designed explicitly towards seizure control purposes amongst patients diagnosed accordingly by healthcare professionals after thorough diagnostic evaluations confirming underlying etiological factors contributing toward recurrent episodes necessitating intervention via appropriate drug therapy strategies
